perturbation.original_text: 4. The Company and AIG agree that any contribution to be made under paragraph 1 will take place within the following two time periods per year, as applicable: (a) during the time beginning on the first business day after the filing of the Company's first fiscal 3 quarterly statutory financial statements and ending on the last business day prior to the end of the Company's second fiscal quarter; and (b) during the time beginning on the first business day after the filing of the Company's third fiscal quarterly statutory financial statements and ending on the last business day prior to the end of the Company's fourth fiscal quarter. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in compliance with applicable law, any capital contribution provided for under paragraph 1 may be made by AIG after the close of any fiscal quarter or fiscal year of the Company but prior to the filing by the Company of its statutory financial statements for such fiscal quarter or fiscal year, respectively, and contributions of this nature shall be recognized as capital contributions receivable as of the balance sheet date of the yet to be filed quarterly or annual financial statement (as the case may be), pursuant to paragraph 8 of Statement of Statutory Accounting Principles No. 72, to the extent approved by the Domiciliary State. The Company and AIG further agree that any dividends to be made under paragraph 2 will take place as soon as practicable after the filing by the Company of the relevant fiscal quarter-end or fiscal year-end statutory financial statements or such earlier time as may be agreed by the Company and AIG.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The Company and AIG agree that any contribution to be made under paragraph 1 will take place within the following two time periods per year, as applicable: (a) during the time beginning on the first business day after the filing of the Company's first fiscal 3 quarterly statutory financial statements and ending on the last business day prior to the end of the Company's second fiscal quarter; and (b) during the time beginning on the first business day after the filing of the Company's third fiscal quarterly statutory financial statements and ending on the last business day prior to the end of the Company's fourth fiscal quarter. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in compliance with applicable law, any capital contribution provided for under paragraph 1 may be made by AIG at any point during the fiscal year of the Company, but prior to the filing by the Company of its statutory financial statements for such fiscal quarter or fiscal year, respectively, and contributions of this nature shall be recognized as capital contributions receivable as of the balance sheet date of the yet to be filed quarterly or annual financial statement (as the case may be), pursuant to paragraph 8 of Statement of Statutory Accounting Principles No. 72, to the extent approved by the Domiciliary State. The Company and AIG further agree that any dividends to be made under paragraph 2 will take place at the end of the fiscal year after the filing by the Company of the relevant fiscal year-end statutory financial statements or at any point as may be agreed by the Company and AIG.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the timing of capital contributions and dividends. The original text specifies two distinct time periods for contributions related to the first/second and third/fourth fiscal quarters, while the changed text allows contributions 'at any point during the fiscal year'. This contradicts the explicit, limited timeframes previously defined. The text also contradict the time dividend will be paid, changing from quaterly to yearly payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. The Company and AIG agree that any contribution to be made under paragraph 1 will take place within the following two time periods per year, as applicable: (a) during the time beginning on the first business day after the filing of the Company's first fiscal 3 quarterly statutory financial statements and ending on the last business day prior to the end of the Company's second fiscal quarter; and (b) during the time beginning on the first business day after the filing of the Company's third fiscal quarterly statutory financial statements and ending on the last business day prior to the end of the Company's fourth fiscal quarter. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in compliance with applicable law, any capital contribution provided for under paragraph 1 may be made by AIG at any point during the fiscal year of the Company, but prior to the filing by the Company of its statutory financial statements for such fiscal quarter or fiscal year, respectively, and contributions of this nature shall be recognized as capital contributions receivable as of the balance sheet date of the yet to be filed quarterly or annual financial statement (as the case may be), pursuant to paragraph 8 of Statement of Statutory Accounting Principles No. 72, to the extent approved by the Domiciliary State. The Company and AIG further agree that any dividends to be made under paragraph 2 will take place at the end of the fiscal year after the filing by the Company of the relevant fiscal year-end statutory financial statements or at any point as may be agreed by the Company and AIG.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies time periods for capital contributions and dividends based on fiscal quarters. The changed text broadens the timeframe for capital contributions to 'any point during the fiscal year' and changes dividend payments to yearly, which contradicts the originally defined quarterly periods for both in paragraph 4. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the timing of these financial activities, hence 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. At the time that any contribution is due under paragraph 4, AIG agrees that it will either (a) make such contribution to the Company's direct parent and cause such direct parent to then contribute such funds, securities or instruments so contributed by AIG to the Company, or (b) make such contribution directly to the Company without receiving any capital stock or other ownership interest in exchange therefor, subject in either case to any required regulatory approvals. At any time any dividends are due under paragraph 4, the Company agrees that it will make such dividend to the Company's direct parent and will use its best efforts to cause such direct parent to then dividend or otherwise provide such funds to AIG. All contributions and dividends contemplated under this Agreement shall be approved, declared and made, as applicable, in compliance with applicable law, including, without limitation, approval by the board of directors of each applicable entity (including the Company) and any prior notice requirements specified under applicable rules and regulations of the Domiciliary State.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. At the time that any contribution is due under paragraph 4, AIG agrees that it will either (a) make such contribution to the Company's direct parent and cause such direct parent to then contribute such funds, securities or instruments so contributed by AIG to the Company, or (b) make such contribution directly to the Company without receiving any capital stock or other ownership interest in exchange therefor, subject in either case to any required regulatory approvals. At any time any dividends are due under paragraph 4, the Company agrees that it will make such dividend payment directly to AIG. All contributions and dividends contemplated under this Agreement shall be approved, declared and made, as applicable, in compliance with applicable law, including, without limitation, approval by the board of directors of each applicable entity (including the Company) and any prior notice requirements specified under applicable rules and regulations of the Domiciliary State.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original flow of dividend payments. Originally, dividends were paid to the Company's direct parent, who would then provide the funds to AIG. The modified text states that the dividends will be paid directly to AIG. The change introduces an in-text contradiction within the section itself. The original flow requires two steps and makes the parent company a mandatory step, while the modified flow removes the parent company from the equation. The text contradiction arises because the process is now made direct, instead of going through the Parent company first.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. At the time that any contribution is due under paragraph 4, AIG agrees that it will either (a) make such contribution to the Company's direct parent and cause such direct parent to then contribute such funds, securities or instruments so contributed by AIG to the Company, or (b) make such contribution directly to the Company without receiving any capital stock or other ownership interest in exchange therefor, subject in either case to any required regulatory approvals. At any time any dividends are due under paragraph 4, the Company agrees that it will make such dividend payment directly to AIG. All contributions and dividends contemplated under this Agreement shall be approved, declared and made, as applicable, in compliance with applicable law, including, without limitation, approval by the board of directors of each applicable entity (including the Company) and any prior notice requirements specified under applicable rules and regulations of the Domiciliary State.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in paragraph 5 directly contradicts the original flow of dividend payments. The original text states that dividends will be paid to the Company's direct parent, who would then provide the funds to AIG. The modified text states that the dividend payment will be made directly to AIG. This introduces an inconsistency within the section itself. Since the contradicted text is the same paragraph, that doesn't mean the contradiction is not present. By changing the dividend path from going to the parent company, and then AIG, to a direct payment to AIG, creates an in-text contradiction according to the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with this paragraph 8, this Agreement shall continue indefinitely. AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale. To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will terminate automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG, and all provisions hereof will be of no further force and effect. For the avoidance of doubt, the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this paragraph 8 shall not relieve either party of any obligation it may owe to the other party hereunder that existed prior to, and remains outstanding as of, the Termination Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with this paragraph 8, this Agreement shall continue indefinitely. AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale. To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will automatically continue indefinitely. For the avoidance of doubt, the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this paragraph 8 shall not relieve either party of any obligation it may owe to the other party hereunder that existed prior to, and remains outstanding as of, the Termination Date.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction within paragraph 8. The original text states that after the sale of the Company by AIG, this agreement will terminate automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG. The changed text states, 'this Agreement will automatically continue indefinitely' creates a direct contradiction. This contradiction creates significant uncertainty, as it is unclear whether the agreement terminates after the sale of the company, or it continues indefinitely. This contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement, because it is unclear if the termination of the agreement is truly happening after the 1 year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with this paragraph 8, this Agreement shall continue indefinitely. AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale. To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will automatically continue indefinitely. For the avoidance of doubt, the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this paragraph 8 shall not relieve either party of any obligation it may owe to the other party hereunder that existed prior to, and remains outstanding as of, the Termination Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that the agreement terminates automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG. The changed text, 'this Agreement will automatically continue indefinitely,' directly conflicts with the original termination clause. The contradicted text is the same location as the changed text. This inconsistency makes it unclear whether the agreement continues indefinitely or terminates after one year, making the obligations unclear and creating potential enforcement issues.
